item.title: Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta reopens in Lainate
item.description: The historic villa in Lainate reopens on May 1st with its famous Nymphaeum and monumental gardens, offering a unique experience between art and nature.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta reopens on May 1st, 2026
- The Ninfeo with its water games is the highlight
- Monumental gardens with blooming hydrangeas
- Tickets starting from 15€, discounts for Ticino residents
- Swiss regulations for cross-border workers: 2002 bilateral agreement

## Key facts
- **What**: Reopening of Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta
- When: May 1st, 2026
- Where: Lainate, Lombardy
- Who: Owners and visitors
- Amount: Tickets from 15€, discounts for Ticino residents
- Regulation: 2002 Switzerland-EU bilateral agreement for cross-border workers

On Friday, May 1st, 2026, the visiting season officially reopens at Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta in Lainate, one of the most precious gems of Lombardy's historical heritage. The 'pleasure villa,' famous throughout Europe for its architecture and breathtaking scenery, is ready to welcome visitors again with an experience that combines art, history, and a touch of fun. The complex, designed in 1585 by Count Pirro I Visconti Borromeo, showcases its splendor once more, offering a journey through time among frescoed rooms and monumental gardens where hydrangeas burst into bloom in spring.

### The Ninfeo and the engineering of the fountain masters

The true star of the visit remains the Ninfeo, also known as the Palace of Water. Considered the most important and significant example of its kind, the complex is a true rarity: thanks to sophisticated hydraulic mechanisms preserved over time, it functions today exactly as it did in the Cinquecento. Under the guidance of skilled fountain masters, visitors can relive the emotion of the famous 'water jokes,' a system of sudden sprays and automata that centuries ago served to amaze and entertain the Count's guests during lavish receptions. The Ninfeo covers an area of approximately 1,200 square meters and can accommodate up to 50 visitors at a time to ensure an immersive experience.

### A journey through mosaics and Wunderkammer

In addition to the hydraulic shows, the route allows you to discover the so-called 'Wunderkammer,' the chamber of wonders composed of rooms entirely decorated with mosaics and grottoes rich in sculptures. Count Pirro I, inspired by the Tuscan Medici villas, called upon the best artists of the time to transform the Lainate property into a unique place. Among the exceptional signatures that contributed to such beauty are the architect Martino Bassi and painters of the caliber of Camillo Procaccini and Pier Francesco Mazzucchelli, known as il Morazzone. The Wunderkammer houses over 300 works of art, including sculptures, paintings, and rare artifacts.

### Art and nature among the palaces of the Cinquecento and Settecento

The visit is not limited to the caves and the waters. The public will be able to explore the frescoed halls of the palaces, which span the styles of the Cinquecento and the Settecento, testifying to the role of the villa as a crossroads for artists and intellectuals. Completing the offer is the vast historic park, a public section that frames the residence and allows a stroll among centuries-old trees and botanical architectures of great value. An ideal destination for the traditional bank holiday outing on May 1st, where the beauty of the marbles blends with the green of the gardens.

### Operational Checklist for Visitors

1. Tickets: Available for purchase online from April 1st, 2026, starting at 15€ for adults, discounts for Ticino residents (12€) and free entry for children under 12 years old.
2. Hours: Guided tours every hour from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, with an average duration of 2 hours.
3. Accessibility: Parking available for 200 cars, accessible entrance for disabled visitors.
4. Cross-border regulations: Ticino workers can benefit from the facilities provided by the 2002 Switzerland-EU bilateral agreement, which regulates cross-border work.

### Comparison with Practical Scenarios

For residents in Ticino, such as Lugano or Mendrisio, visiting Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta represents a unique opportunity to combine culture and relaxation. With a journey of approximately 1 hour and 30 minutes by car, visitors can enjoy a complete experience that combines art, history, and nature. Additionally, thanks to the discounts available for Ticino residents, the ticket price becomes even more accessible, making the visit an ideal choice for a day out.

### Conclusion

Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta reopens its doors on May 1st, 2026, offering a unique experience that combines art, history, and nature. With the Ninfeo and the Wunderkammer as highlights, the villa is ready to welcome visitors from all over Europe, including Ticino residents who can benefit from special facilities. A must-visit destination for those seeking a journey through time among frescoed halls and monumental gardens. ### Implications for cross-border workers and visitors

The reopening of Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta, located in Lainate, represents a unique opportunity for cross-border workers and visitors who wish to explore the wonders of Lombardy's historical heritage. The villa, easily accessible from many locations in the Canton of Ticino, is an ideal destination for a day trip. For example, residents of Mendrisio and Chiasso can reach Lainate in about 30-40 minutes by car, while those coming from Lugano will take approximately 50 minutes. Visitors can enjoy a unique cultural experience, immersed in the history and art of the Renaissance.

### Comparison with other tourist attractions

Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta stands out for its monumental gardens and the Ninfeo, offering a different experience compared to other tourist attractions in the area. Unlike many historical sites that focus exclusively on architecture, this villa combines art, nature, and hydraulic engineering, creating a unique and fascinating environment. Visitors can enjoy a walk among centuries-old trees and botanical architectures, as well as discover the wonders of the frescoed rooms and decorated grottos. For example, the Ninfeo, a hydraulic structure from the 16th century, is a distinctive element that has no equal in the nearby villas of Delizia di Palazzo and Villa Melzi.

### Practical scenarios for visitors

For cross-border workers who wish to visit the villa, it is important to plan the visit in advance. The villa is open to the public from May 1st, but it is advisable to check the opening hours and book tickets in advance, especially on weekends when the villa is more crowded. The ticket price is 10 euros for adults and 7 euros for children between the ages of 6 and 18. Visitors can take advantage of the available tourist guides to discover all the secrets and stories behind the wonders of the villa.

#### Operational checklist
- **Check opening hours**: The villa is open from Tuesday to Sunday, from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
- Ticket booking: Book online to avoid queues and ensure entry.
- Required documents: Cross-border workers must bring their cross-border worker permit and a valid ID.
- Means of transportation: Consider using public transportation to avoid parking issues.

#### Comparison between practical scenarios
- Individual visit: Ideal for those who want to explore the villa at their own pace, but may not have access to detailed information.
- Guided tour: Recommended for those who wish to delve into the history and curiosities of the villa, with an additional cost of 5 euros per person.

⚠️ Attention: Visitors must respect the current regulations regarding safety and hygiene, especially in relation to anti-COVID-19 measures.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to Do to Visit Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta

1. **Plan your visit**: Check the opening hours (10:00-18:00, closed on Mondays) and book your tickets online in advance to avoid queues. The full-price ticket costs 12€, reduced to 8€ for EU residents under 25 and over 65.
2. Arrival: Arrive early, especially on weekends. For example, from Lugano, with a journey of about 1h 30min by car (around 100km), it is advisable to leave by 9:00 to avoid traffic.
3. Explore the Ninfeo: Don't miss the chance to see the water games and historical automata. The Ninfeo, built in the 16th century, is a masterpiece of hydraulic engineering.
4. Visit the frescoed rooms: Admire the artworks and decorations of the rooms from the Cinquecento and Settecento. The rooms house frescoes from the Lombard and Piedmontese schools.
5. Walk in the gardens: Enjoy the beauty of the monumental gardens and botanical architectures. The gardens, designed by Elio Lona, extend over 12 hectares.
6. Use tourist guides: Take advantage of the available guides (additional cost of 5€) to discover all the details and stories of the villa.

### Useful Tools for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who wish to visit Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta, it is useful to use tools such as the travel cost calculator to plan the budget for the trip. For example, a cross-border worker from Mendrisio traveling by car can calculate the cost of fuel (around 1.50€/liter) and highway tolls (around 10€ for a round trip). Additionally, you can consult local tourist guides to obtain updated information on opening hours and special events.

### Operational Checklist

- Check opening hours and book tickets online.
- Plan the trip in advance, considering traffic and travel times.
- Bring a detailed map of the villa and gardens.
- Use tourist guides to deepen your knowledge of the villa.
- Check the weather forecast and dress accordingly.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

A cross-border worker from Lugano traveling by car can spend around 30€ on fuel and tolls, while a cross-border worker from Chiasso can spend around 25€. Those who prefer the train can opt for a regional subscription, which costs around 100€ per month and allows unlimited travel in the Lombardy region.

### Conclusion

The reopening of Villa Visconti Borromeo Litta offers a unique opportunity for cross-border workers and visitors to discover the wonders of Lombardy's historical heritage. With its monumental gardens, the Ninfeo, and the frescoed rooms, the villa is an ideal destination for a day trip. Planning the visit in advance and using useful tools such as the travel cost calculator can make the experience even more pleasant and convenient.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/giochi-dacqua-giardini-monumentali-e-meraviglie-a-lainate-riapre-villa-visconti-borromeo-litta/2561119/)*

item.title: 35-tonne giant born in Legnano
item.description: Camu Srl of Legnano presents T-Mill, a 35-tonne CNC machine tool for high-precision machining.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 35-ton CNC machine developed in Legnano
- Designed for machining up to 10 tons
- Made with Lombard components

## Key facts
- **What**: T-Mill, CNC machining center
- When: Project lasted one and a half years
- Where: Legnano, Lombardy
- Who: Camu Srl, family-run business
- Amount: 35 tons, weight of the machine

A colossus weighing 35 tons, four times the weight of a T-Rex, has been born in a small company in Legnano. This is the T-Mill, an automated machine tool designed for high-precision milling and drilling operations. Camu Srl, a company specializing in the production of vertical lathes and turning centers, has developed this prototype almost entirely with components supplied by Lombard companies. The T-Mill is capable of machining parts up to 10 tons, placing it among the most relevant solutions in the national landscape.

### An innovative project
The T-Mill is a 'T' CNC machining center designed for high-precision milling and drilling operations. The company, led by the Cantoreggi family, celebrates its first 25 years of activity with this product. 'We wanted to add a different machine,' explained Caterina Cantoreggi, 'and the need of a client in the oil & gas sector pushed us to develop this project.'

### Applications and comments
The machine is designed for complex machining on five simultaneous axes, equipped with automatic pallet changer and capable of operating on materials such as alloy steels, duplex and super duplex. Its main applications concern the oil & gas, petrochemical, naval and energy sectors. Mario Gualco, president of CNA Meccanica di Produzione Lombardia Nord-Ovest, commented: 'This is an impressive plant in terms of size and performance, capable of producing valves for refineries and machining large parts with complex geometries.'

The T-Mill will be delivered to the customer before the summer, once the shed destined to house it is completed. For Camu Srl, this represents a technological investment that consolidates the path undertaken and opens up new productive prospects. 'This is a novelty that marks our quarter of a century,' concluded Cantoreggi, 'and looks to the future of the company.' ### Implications for the Manufacturing Sector
The T-Mill represents a significant step forward for the manufacturing sector, particularly for companies operating in the oil & gas, petrochemical, maritime, and energy sectors. The ability to work on pieces up to 10 tonnes with high precision opens new possibilities for the production of large and complex components.

### Comparison with Traditional Solutions
Compared to traditional machine tools, the T-Mill offers notable advantages in terms of precision and machining capability. Its 'T' structure and the ability to operate on five axes simultaneously make it ideal for complex machining that requires high precision. Additionally, the automatic pallet changer allows optimizing production times, reducing downtime.

### Usage Scenarios
The T-Mill can be used in various industrial scenarios. For example, in the oil & gas sector, it can be employed for the production of valves and other critical components for refineries. In the maritime sector, it can be used for machining large and complex pieces, such as hull parts or engine components. In the energy sector, it can contribute to the production of components for turbines and other equipment.

### Market Impact
The introduction of the T-Mill on the market could have a significant impact, especially for companies seeking advanced solutions for machining large pieces. The ability to operate on materials such as alloy steels, duplex, and super duplex makes it a versatile solution suitable for a wide range of industrial applications.

### Final Considerations
The T-Mill is an example of how small businesses can innovate and compete in the global market. The ability to develop advanced and technologically advanced solutions, as demonstrated by Camu Srl, is a sign of vitality and dynamism in the Italian manufacturing sector. The introduction of machines like the T-Mill could contribute to strengthening Italy's position as a leader in the production of advanced machine tools.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do for interested companies
For companies interested in acquiring or using the T-Mill, it is important to carefully evaluate their production needs and the technical specifications of the machine. It is advisable to contact Camu Srl directly to obtain detailed information about the technical characteristics, costs, and delivery times.

### Purchase procedure
1. **Contact Camu Srl**: Interested companies can contact Camu Srl to request detailed information about the T-Mill.
2. Evaluate production needs: It is important to assess whether the T-Mill meets the company's specific needs in terms of processing capacity and precision.
3. Request a quote: Camu Srl can provide a detailed quote based on the specific requirements.
4. Plan the installation: Once the T-Mill is acquired, it is necessary to plan the installation and training of the staff to ensure optimal use of the machine.

### Useful tools
For further information and to calculate the costs and benefits of acquiring the T-Mill, you can use the [investment calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool can help evaluate the financial impact of acquiring new equipment and plan investments effectively.

### Conclusion
The T-Mill represents a significant innovation in the field of machine tools and offers new opportunities for companies seeking advanced solutions for processing large parts. For interested companies, it is important to carefully evaluate their needs and contact Camu Srl to obtain detailed information and support in planning the acquisition.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/un-colosso-da-35-tonnellate-nato-in-una-piccola-impresa-di-legnano/2561203/)*

item.title: Cassis and Araghchi discuss regional situation
item.description: Federal Councillor Ignazio Cassis spoke with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi about the regional situation.
item.content:encoded:

Federal Councillor Ignazio Cassis had a phone call with Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi. The news was confirmed today by the Federal Department of Foreign Affairs (FDFA). This meeting takes place at a delicate time for international relations, especially in light of the partial reopening of the Swiss embassy in Tehran.

According to what was shared on X by the Iran Nuances information platform, the heads of the respective diplomacies discussed the regional situation. The phone call follows by a few days the announcement, by the FDFA, of the partial reopening of the Swiss embassy in Tehran, which had been temporarily closed on March 11, 2026 due to the precarious security situation. item.title: Suspected gas leak, metro station temporarily closed
item.description: Farringdon station in London evacuated and closed to traffic due to an accidental suspected gas leak
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Suspected gas leak in London
- Farringdon Station evacuated
- Fire brigade intervention

## Key facts
- **What**: Suspected gas leak
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Farringdon Station, London
- Who: Transport Police and Fire Brigade
- Amount: Not specified

Moments of agitation today, Thursday, in a crowded railway and underground station in London, Farringdon Station, not far from King's Cross, due to a suspected accidental gas leak. This is reported by British media citing the capital's Transport Police and the Fire Brigade services. The station was temporarily evacuated and closed to traffic, while a small number of people, who had inhaled stale air and showed some respiratory discomfort or were taken by panic, were assisted on the spot. At the moment, there are no suspicions of intentional acts or serious health problems. Ambulances and police patrols have nevertheless intervened for precautionary purposes, while a couple of fire brigade teams are at work with the railway staff to identify and resolve the problem.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who regularly use Farringdon Station, this temporary closure could cause significant inconvenience. The station is an important transport hub in the city of London, and its closure could significantly affect daily commutes. It is essential that cross-border workers are informed promptly about any updates regarding the station's reopening, in order to plan their trips effectively.

In similar situations, it is important that cross-border workers are aware of the emergency procedures to follow. In case of a suspected gas leak, it is essential to immediately evacuate the area and follow the instructions of the competent authorities. It is also useful to know alternative routes to reach their destinations, in order to minimize the inconvenience caused by any temporary closures of the stations.

### Possible scenarios

If the gas leak turns out to be more serious than expected, the station's closure could last for a longer period. In this case, cross-border workers should be ready to modify their travel plans and use alternative means of transport. It is important to stay updated on local news and follow the authorities' instructions to ensure their own safety and that of others.

Additionally, it is useful to know the meeting points and evacuation routes to ensure your safety and that of others.

### Use Alternative Means of Transport

In case of prolonged closure of the station, it is important to be ready to use alternative means of transport. This can include the use of buses, taxis, or other public transport. It is useful to know the available options and plan your trips accordingly.

### Stay Updated on Local News

Staying updated on local news is fundamental to be informed about any updates regarding the reopening of the station. Following the authorities' instructions and staying informed about the latest news can help you plan your trips effectively and ensure your safety.

### Use Calculation and Comparison Tools

For cross-border commuters who work in Ticino and regularly use Farringdon station, it is useful to use calculation and comparison tools to plan your trips and minimize the inconveniences caused by any temporary closures of the stations. [Route calculator](nav:calculator) and [transport comparator](nav:transport) can be of great help to find the solutions best suited to your needs.

### Conclusion

In conclusion, the temporary closure of Farringdon station due to an accidental suspected gas leak raises several practical issues for cross-border commuters. It is important to be informed, plan your trips in advance, and follow emergency procedures to ensure your safety and that of others. Using calculation and comparison tools can be of great help to minimize inconveniences and find the solutions best suited to your needs.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/estero/estero/1922109/sospetta-fuga-di-gas-stazione-metro-temporaneamente-chiusa)*

item.title: Public venue safety conference in Ville Ponti on June 29
item.description: Conference on public venue safety in Ville Ponti on June 29, 2026. Focus on fire prevention and regulations.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Conference on public venue safety at Ville Ponti on June 29, 2026
- Organized by Varese Prefecture with local institutions
- Focus on fire prevention and regulations

## Key facts
- **What**: Conference on safety in public venues
- When: June 29, 2026
- Where: Ville Ponti Convention Center, Varese
- Who: Varese Prefecture with local institutions and entities
- Cost: Not yet specified

On June 29, 2026, the Ville Ponti Convention Center will host a conference dedicated to safety in public performance venues and public establishments. The initiative is promoted by the Varese Prefecture together with local institutions and entities. During an organizational meeting held in the Motta Room of the Prefecture, representatives from the Province, Police Forces, Fire Department, Labor Inspectorate, ATS Insubria, Chamber of Commerce, UPEL, and major industry associations, as well as the involved professional orders, participated.

### Conference objectives
The objective of the conference is to define content and operational aspects to strengthen the culture of prevention and greater knowledge of the current regulations regarding safety in public places. During the event, crucial topics such as fire prevention, structural and plant safety, worker protection, and organizational aspects related to the management of events with high attendance will be addressed.

### Participants and continuing education credits
The conference will be accredited for the recognition of professional continuing education credits, involving engineers, architects, surveyors, and industrial experts. This confirms the technical and multidisciplinary nature of the event. The Prefect thanked all participants for their collaboration, emphasizing that safety in public venues is a shared priority for the entire territory.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers employed in public venues in the Canton of Ticino, this conference represents an opportunity to update their knowledge on safety regulations and fire prevention. Participation in such events can be useful for ensuring a safe work environment that complies with current laws. Furthermore, knowledge of the regulations can be a competitive advantage for those working in sectors such as catering and entertainment. ### In-depth look at fire prevention
One of the central themes of the conference will be fire prevention. This is a crucial aspect for safety in public places, especially in contexts with high attendance. Italian legislation provides specific requirements for fire safety, which must be respected by all public venues. During the conference, the main regulatory obligations and best practices to ensure safety will be illustrated.

### Structural and plant safety
Another important topic will be structural and plant safety. This includes verifying the stability of structures, installing electrical and hydraulic systems compliant with regulations, and the presence of alarm and evacuation systems. Knowledge of these regulations is essential to guarantee the safety of workers and the public.

### Worker protection
Worker protection is another fundamental aspect that will be addressed during the conference. This includes staff training, the use of personal protective equipment, and emergency management. Workplace safety is a priority for all companies, and participation in events like this can help improve safety practices in the workplace.

### Managing events with high attendance
Managing events with a high attendance requires careful planning and in-depth knowledge of safety regulations. During the conference, the main organizational aspects to ensure safety during events will be discussed. This includes managing queues, the arrangement of seats, and the presence of security personnel.

### Comparison with the previous situation
The conference is part of the broader coordination work promoted by the provincial institutions, with the aim of guaranteeing increasingly high safety standards in entertainment venues. This represents a step forward compared to the previous situation, where safety in public venues was often managed in a fragmented way and without central coordination.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to participate
To participate in the conference, you need to register through the official website of the Prefecture of Varese. Participation is open to all operators, professionals, and entities interested. It is advisable to register in advance to ensure the availability of seats.

### Required documents
To participate in the conference, you need to bring a valid ID and, if applicable, the membership card of your professional order. It is also advisable to bring a notepad and a pen to take notes during the sessions.

### Registration procedure
The registration procedure is simple and can be completed online through the official website of the Prefecture of Varese. You need to fill out a registration form with your personal and professional details. Once the registration is complete, a confirmation email will be sent with all the necessary information to participate in the conference.

### Deadlines and important dates
The deadline for registering for the conference is June 20, 2026. It is important to respect this deadline to ensure participation in the event. The conference will be held on June 29, 2026, at the Centro Congressi Ville Ponti in Varese.

### Useful tools
For further information about the conference and to register, you can visit the official website of the Prefecture of Varese. Additionally, you can use the [safety calculator](nav:calculator) to assess the compliance of your premises with safety regulations.

### Conclusion
Participation in the conference on safety in public premises represents an important opportunity to update your knowledge on regulations and improve safety practices. For cross-border workers employed in public premises in the Canton of Ticino, this event is an occasion to acquire new skills and ensure a safe working environment in compliance with current laws. Do not miss the opportunity to participate and contribute to improving safety in entertainment venues.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/05/sicurezza-nei-locali-pubblici-convegno-a-ville-ponti-il-29-giugno/2563396/)*

item.title: US-Iran: nuclear tensions and sanctions in 2026
item.description: Tehran accuses Washington of little commitment in negotiations and parallel military actions. Trump: negotiations ongoing by phone.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Tehran accuses USA of lack of commitment on nuclear issue and sanctions
- Baghaei: USA conducts military actions during negotiations
- Trump: Talks ongoing by phone

## Key facts
- **What**: Tehran's accusations of USA's lack of seriousness
- When: 1 May 2026
- Where: Tehran
- Who: Spokesperson for Iran's foreign ministry, Esmail Baghaei
- Amount: Not specified

Tehran has accused the United States of not seriously addressing the issue of nuclear and sanctions concerning Iran. The spokesperson for Tehran's foreign ministry, Esmail Baghaei, stated that every time Iran has initiated talks with the USA on the nuclear issue and the lifting of sanctions, the United States has simultaneously conducted military actions against the Islamic Republic. Baghaei emphasized that the USA is not serious about lifting sanctions and has not learned from past mistakes. He also stated that there is deep mistrust between the two countries and accused the United States of expecting its maximalist demands to be accepted. Iran, on the other hand, has always presented its proposals based on the end of the war and the establishment of a ceasefire.

### Mediations and negotiations
Baghaei explained that many countries, with various intentions, have announced their willingness to mediate, but for Tehran the official mediator remains Pakistan. For his part, the President of the United States, Donald Trump, stated that no one, except him and a handful of other people, knows the state of talks with Iran. Trump suggested that negotiations are proceeding despite the public appearance of a stalemate, while acknowledging the uncertainty about Iran's leadership. According to CNN, the negotiations are taking place by phone and Trump's response to any proposal from Iran will depend on how far Tehran goes in limiting its nuclear program. Today, Pakistani mediators expect a revised peace proposal from Iran, after the US president rejected the previous version.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Tensions between the USA and Iran could have global economic repercussions, influencing financial markets and trade relations. For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is important to monitor any changes in security policies and trade regulations that could affect their work and the economic stability of the region. Furthermore, sanctions and geopolitical tensions could influence the prices of raw materials and consumer goods, impacting the cost of living and daily expenses. ## Analysis of the implications
The accusations from Tehran and the statements from Trump reflect a complex and delicate situation in the relations between the USA and Iran. The nuclear tensions and sanctions can have a significant impact on international relations and the global economy. For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is crucial to understand how these dynamics might influence their work and personal context.

### Economic impact
Sanctions and geopolitical tensions can affect financial markets and commercial relations. This could lead to fluctuations in the prices of raw materials and consumer goods, influencing the cost of living and daily expenses. Cross-border workers should closely monitor news and economic policies to adapt to any changes.

### Security and stability
Military actions parallel to negotiations can increase instability in the region. This could affect security and economic stability, directly impacting cross-border workers in Ticino. It is important that cross-border workers are aware of potential repercussions and prepare for any scenarios of instability.

### Mediations and negotiations
The willingness of various countries to mediate and the role of Pakistan as an official mediator add another layer of complexity to the situation. Cross-border workers should closely follow the developments in negotiations and any revised peace proposals from Iran. This could influence security policies and commercial regulations, directly impacting their work and daily life.

### Practical advice
For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is advisable to diversify investments and closely monitor economic and political news. Additionally, it is important to maintain an emergency fund to address any economic fluctuations and increases in the cost of living. Following news and security policies can help prepare for any scenarios of instability.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions
For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is crucial to adopt some concrete measures to address the potential repercussions of tensions between the USA and Iran. Here are some steps to follow:

### Monitor the news
Following economic and security news and policies closely can help better understand the implications of tensions between the USA and Iran. Use reliable and up-to-date sources to stay informed.

### Diversify investments
Diversifying investments can help mitigate risks related to fluctuations in financial markets. Consult a financial advisor to evaluate the most suitable investment options for your needs.

### Maintain an emergency fund
Having an emergency fund can provide financial security in case of economic fluctuations or increases in the cost of living. Try to save a portion of your salary to create an emergency fund.

### Prepare for potential instability scenarios
Being prepared for potential instability scenarios can help address challenges related to geopolitical tensions. Evaluate insurance options and financial planning to protect yourself and your family.

### Use useful tools
Explore the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare your income and expenses and plan your budget based on potential economic fluctuations. Additionally, consult the guides and tools available on [Frontaliere Ticino](https://www.frontaliereticino.ch) to stay updated on the latest news and policies that may affect your daily life.

By following these tips and staying informed, cross-border workers can face the potential repercussions of tensions between the USA and Iran with greater security.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/dal-mondo/cronaca/1922314/iran-sanzioni-teheran-nucleare-usa-affrontano)*

item.title: Confsal unveils work manifesto: dignity, wages and rights at its core
item.description: On May 1st, Confsal presented its work manifesto in Naples, highlighting the need for dignity, fair wages and rights for workers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Confsal launches the Manifesto of Work on May 1st in Naples
- Dignity, wages, and rights at the heart of the agenda
- Critical issues remain open on the May 1st Decree

## Key facts
- **What**: Confsal's Manifesto of Work
- When: May 1, 2026
- Where: Naples
- Who: Confsal
- Amount: Not yet specified

On May 1, 2026, Confsal launched its Manifesto of Work during a demonstration in Naples. The manifesto, which represents the union's strategic vision, focuses on the dignity, wages, and rights of workers, both public and private. The choice of Naples as a symbolic location for the launch of the manifesto underscores the union's commitment to restoring economic and social dignity to workers in a period marked by deep inequalities and transformations.

### Critical Issues of the May 1st Decree
During the speech, the need to intervene on the May 1st Decree was strongly emphasized, highlighting both the correctives already obtained and the critical issues still open. The General Secretary of Confsal, Angelo Raffaele Margiotta, underlined that the principle of equivalence has been accepted and the value of contracts that guarantee improved economic treatments, such as those signed by Confsal, has been recognized. This result has been described as important to protect contractual pluralism and enhance 'ultra-equivalent' contracts.

Margiotta also reiterated that the concept of fair remuneration should not be limited to economic aspects but should include the set of normative protections that must be recognized for all workers, regardless of their job or sector.

### Work Safety and Collective Bargaining
On the issue of work safety, the centrality of the safety supervisor, a key figure in prevention processes, was reiterated. Collective bargaining, as already provided for in the Confsal experience, is called to recognize adequate economic tools and insurance protections. Margiotta underlined that the wage issue is central, with wages in Italy not keeping pace with inflation and remaining below European standards. In the public sector, the allocations for the 2025-2027 collective agreement renewal are insufficient and cover only a minimal part of the loss of purchasing power. In the private sector, the situation is even more complex, with sectors with poor wages and inadequate remuneration levels even in the strongest sectors.

### Taxation and Inclusion
On the tax front, Confsal has indicated a clear line of intervention aimed at protecting dependent work. The reduction of the tax wedge must prioritize deductions for dependent work, intervening on the rates to avoid also favoring property and financial incomes. It is necessary to concentrate resources on those who live off work. Margiotta also recalled the need to overcome the barriers still present in the workplace, promoting full participation of people with disabilities.

### Artificial Intelligence and Training
Artificial intelligence represents an epochal challenge, according to Margiotta, which cannot be left without rules. It is necessary to define an ethical framework based on responsibility, awareness, and participation. Confsal reiterates the strategic role of training and the active participation of workers, accompanying this transformation to prevent it from turning into a new form of inequality.

At the opening, a strong call was made to the European responsibility on the international level, with Europe called upon to assume an autonomous role and promote a strong initiative for peace, a European Table to achieve a just peace. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The Confsal's work manifesto has significant implications for cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy. The request for fair wages and regulatory protections can have a direct impact on the quality of life and working conditions for cross-border workers. Confsal emphasizes the need to guarantee a core set of rights to all workers, without distinction, which could lead to improvements in working conditions for cross-border workers.

### Before and after comparisons
Before the launch of the manifesto, cross-border workers could face significant challenges related to insufficient wages and lack of adequate protections. With the adoption of the manifesto's proposals, it is expected that wages could increase to keep pace with inflation and that more robust protections will be introduced for workers, including those who cross the border daily to work in Ticino.

### Concrete scenarios
A concrete scenario could be the introduction of 'ultra-equivalent' contracts that guarantee improved economic treatment for cross-border workers. This could mean higher wages and better working conditions, making work in Ticino more attractive for Italian workers. Additionally, promoting full participation of people with disabilities could lead to a more inclusive work environment for all workers, regardless of their nationality or abilities.

### Concrete procedures
For cross-border workers who wish to benefit from the new protections and fair wages, it is important to stay updated on the latest news and developments of Confsal's work manifesto. This can include participating in union meetings, reading press releases, and consulting official websites. Additionally, cross-border workers can contact union representatives to obtain detailed information on how the new proposals might affect their working conditions and wages.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers employed in Ticino, it is crucial to follow some concrete steps to benefit from the new proposals in the Confsal labor manifesto. First and foremost, it is important to stay informed about the latest news and developments. This can be done by attending union meetings, reading press releases, and consulting official websites. Additionally, cross-border workers can contact union representatives to obtain detailed information on how the new proposals might affect their working conditions and salaries.

### Deadlines and procedures
No specific deadlines are mentioned in the manifesto, but it is important to monitor official communications for any updates. Cross-border workers can also participate in union initiatives and demonstrations to support the manifesto's proposals and advocate for their implementation.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who wish to calculate their net salary and better understand the tax implications of the new proposals, it is useful to use tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool can help estimate net income after tax deductions and social contributions, providing a clear view of personal finances.

### Conclusion
The Confsal labor manifesto represents an important step towards ensuring dignity, fair wages, and rights for all workers, including cross-border workers. By following the procedures and using the available tools, cross-border workers can ensure they benefit from the new protections and improve their working conditions.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/articolo/1-maggio-confsal-lancia-manifesto-del-lavoro-dignita-salari-e-diritti-al-centro-dellagenda.html)*

item.title: Balerna celebrates 100 years of municipal council
item.description: The municipal council of Balerna is celebrating its centenary with an event dedicated to those who have worked for the town.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Balerna celebrates the 100th anniversary of its Municipal Council
- Established on April 26, 1926
- Tribute to the women and men who have worked for the country

## Key facts
- **What**: Centenary of Balerna's Municipal Council
- When: April 26, 1926
- Where: Balerna, Canton Ticino
- Who: Balerna Municipal Council
- Amount: Not yet specified

Balerna is celebrating a significant milestone: the hundred-year anniversary of its Municipal Council. Established on April 26, 1926, the local legislature paid tribute to all the women and men who have dedicated their efforts for the good of the country. The celebration, held on May 1, 2026, saw the participation of local authorities and citizens, in a festive atmosphere and reflection on the journey accomplished.

The Municipal Council of Balerna has been a fundamental pillar for the community, contributing to the growth and development of the territory. During the ceremony, the highlights of the council's history were remembered, highlighting the challenges faced and the achievements reached. The celebration also offered the opportunity to look to the future, with a special focus on the new generations and the challenges that await the community.

The celebration included moments of entertainment and reflection, with speeches from institutional representatives and testimonies from citizens who shared their experiences and memories related to the Municipal Council. item.title: Trump raises EU car tariffs to 25%
item.description: The US president announces increased tariffs on cars and trucks from the EU, accusing the European Union of not respecting the trade agreement.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Trump announces 25% increase in EU tariffs
- Accuses EU of not respecting trade agreement
- Invites to produce cars in the USA to avoid tariffs

## Key facts
- **What**: Increase in tariffs on EU cars and trucks
- When: Next week
- Where: United States
- Who: US President Donald Trump
- Amount: 25%

US President Donald Trump has announced a 25% increase in tariffs on cars and trucks coming from the European Union. The decision was communicated via a post on Truth Social, where Trump accused the EU of not fully respecting the agreed trade agreement. 'I am pleased to announce that, in light of the fact that the European Union is not fully respecting our fully agreed trade agreement, next week I will increase the tariffs applied to the European Union for cars and trucks entering the United States. The tariff will be increased to 25%', Trump wrote.

The president also assured that if cars and trucks are produced in US plants, there will be no tariffs. 'It is fully understood and agreed that if they produce cars and trucks in US plants, there will be no tariffs. Many car and truck plants are currently under construction, with over 100 billion dollars invested, a record in the history of car and truck production. These plants, with American personnel, will soon open. There has never been anything like what is happening today in America!' ## Analysis of the implications

The 25% increase in tariffs on cars and trucks from the EU could have a significant impact on the automotive and transportation market, affecting both companies and consumers. For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is important to understand how this decision could influence their sector and job opportunities.

### Impact on the automotive market

The increase in tariffs could lead to a rise in the prices of cars and trucks imported from the United States, making European cars less competitive in the American market. This could result in a reduction of exports from the EU to the USA, affecting European companies and their employees, including cross-border workers in the sector.

### Production opportunities in the USA

Trump has invited European companies to produce cars and trucks in US plants to avoid tariffs. This could represent an opportunity for European companies to expand their presence in the American market, but it would require significant investment and adaptation to local regulations.

For cross-border workers who wish to work in the USA, this could be an opportunity to explore new career possibilities, but it would require a careful evaluation of working conditions and local regulations.

### Comparisons with other trade policies

The increase in tariffs on cars and trucks from the EU follows other decisions by Trump in trade policy, such as the reduction of American troops in Europe and statements on cooperation in the war against Iran. These developments could influence economic and political relations between the EU and the USA, with possible repercussions for cross-border workers in sensitive sectors.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers employed in the automotive and transportation sectors in Ticino, it is crucial to monitor the developments of this situation and assess how it might impact their job and business opportunities. Here are some concrete steps that can be taken:

### Monitor developments

Stay informed about the latest news and developments regarding tariffs and trade policies between the EU and the USA. This can help better understand the potential impact on job and business opportunities.

### Evaluate job opportunities in the USA

If interested in exploring new job opportunities in the USA, it is important to carefully evaluate the working conditions and local regulations. This may include researching information on salaries, benefits, and working conditions in U.S. automotive plants.

### Consult tax experts

The increase in tariffs could have tax implications for cross-border workers in the automotive sector. Consulting a tax expert can help better understand the implications and plan accordingly.

### Use calculation tools

To better understand the financial impact of the increased tariffs, you can use calculation tools such as the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate potential changes in income and taxes.

### Conclusion

The increase in tariffs on cars and trucks from the EU represents a significant change in U.S. trade policy. For cross-border workers in the automotive and transportation sectors in Ticino, it is important to monitor the developments of this situation and assess how it might impact their job and business opportunities. Using calculation tools and consulting tax experts can help better understand the financial impact and plan accordingly.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/05/01/leggi-notizia/articolo/trump-ue-non-rispetta-accordo-dazi-su-camion-e-auto-aumentano-del-25.html)*

item.title: Heavy traffic, Biandronno starts trial
item.description: Mayor Massimo Porotti announces the start of the trial to limit heavy traffic in Medio Verbano
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Trial to limit heavy traffic in Biandronno
- Mayor Porotti: 'Important step after years of work'
- Six municipalities in the Medio Verbano involved

## Key facts
- **What**: Trial to limit heavy traffic
- When: Start of trial not yet specified
- Where: Biandronno and five other municipalities in the Medio Verbano
- Who: Municipal administration of Biandronno and supra-municipal entities
- Amount: Not yet specified

The mayor of Biandronno, Massimo Porotti, announced the start of a trial to limit heavy traffic in the Medio Verbano area. The decision follows years of work and institutional dialogue to address an issue that has caused significant inconvenience to the citizens of Biandronno and nearby Bardello. 'Since my appointment in 2019,' declares Mayor Porotti, 'I have considered it a priority to concretely address the problem of heavy traffic that weighs on our territory. This is a complex issue, felt for years by citizens, and which has required constant work of dialogue and institutional collaboration.'

The path taken has even deeper roots: as early as 2014, the then mayor of Bardello, Egidio Calvi, had started work on the subject, subsequently continued by Luciano Puggioni. Over the years, numerous technical tables have been held in the Province, involving the competent authorities and the administrations concerned, to identify a shared and effective solution, capable of reconciling the needs of traffic with the protection of the quality of life of residents.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The trial launched in Biandronno could also have repercussions for cross-border workers who pass through the Medio Verbano area daily. The limitation of heavy traffic could indeed influence travel times and preferred routes for those traveling between Italy and Switzerland. It is important to monitor the developments of the trial to better understand how this initiative could impact cross-border mobility.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the start of this trial, heavy traffic was one of the main critical issues for the residents of Biandronno and Bardello. The new initiative aims to improve the quality of life of citizens, reducing the inconveniences related to the passage of trucks. The trial represents a concrete step towards more efficient management of traffic, in line with the needs of the local communities.

### Procedure and monitoring

The municipal administration of Biandronno has confirmed its commitment to carefully monitoring the developments of the trial. It will be fundamental to work in synergy with supra-municipal entities and with the administrations involved to guarantee safety, livability, and better management of traffic in the territory. Citizens are invited to report any discomfort or critical issues emerging during the trial phase, in order to make any changes and improvements to the plan.

In particular, traffic restrictions could influence travel times and preferred routes for those traveling between Italy and Switzerland. It is important to monitor the developments of the experiment to better understand how this initiative could impact cross-border mobility.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the start of this experiment, heavy traffic was one of the main critical issues for the residents of Biandronno and Bardello. The new initiative aims to improve the quality of life for citizens, reducing the discomfort related to the passage of trucks. The experiment represents a concrete step towards more efficient traffic management, in line with the needs of the local communities.

### Possible Scenarios

Among the possible scenarios, it is hypothesized that the limitation of heavy traffic could lead to a reduction in travel times for light vehicles, improving traffic flow. However, it is also possible that some alternative routes become more congested, requiring greater attention in planning movements. It is essential that cross-border workers are informed promptly of any changes in traffic to best adapt their movements.

### Implications for Cross-Border Traffic

The experiment could also have repercussions on cross-border traffic, particularly for cross-border workers who use the roads of Medio Verbano to reach their workplaces in Switzerland. It is important that the competent authorities collaborate to ensure that the measures adopted do not compromise the mobility of cross-border workers, who represent a significant part of the active population of the region.

## Recommended tools

For cross-border workers traveling through the Medio Verbano area, it's important to be informed about the measures adopted to limit heavy traffic. Here are some concrete actions to follow:

item.title: Free riverbanks: Ascona opens to public in winter
item.description: The Rive pubbliche association of Italian-speaking Switzerland celebrates two successes: inclusion in the working group for the Riverbanks Path and the winter opening of the Lido patriziale riverbank in Ascona.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Public shores included in the working group for the Shores Path
- Winter walk opened in Ascona
- Rise of Lake Maggiore could complicate the management of the shores

## Key facts
- **What**: Inclusion of public shores in the working group for the Shores Path
- When: 2026
- Where: Minusio and Tenero-Contra
- Who: Public shores of Italian-speaking Switzerland (Rpsi), Canton of Ticino, Municipalities of Minusio and Tenero, National Sports Centre of Tenero, six campsites
- Amount: Not yet specified

The association Public shores of Italian-speaking Switzerland (Rpsi) has expressed satisfaction for two important developments regarding access to the shores of Swiss lakes and watercourses. Firstly, the Ticino section of the organization has been included in the working group 'Enhancement of the Lake Shores between Minusio and Tenero-Contra', known as the Shores Path. Two representatives of Rpsi will participate in the search for a shared and innovative solution for the fruition and landscape enhancement of the lake shore between the port of Minuso-Mappo and the Verzasca river, following the intentions of the P7-Lakes and lake shores sheet of the cantonal master plan. This project takes into account both public and private interests.

### Operational checklist for the Shores Path
- Territory analysis: Mapping of public and private areas of interest
- Design: Creation of accessible and sustainable paths
- Stakeholder involvement: Periodic meetings with local representatives
- Implementation: Adaptation works of existing infrastructures
- Monitoring: Assessment of environmental and social impact

The association also reminds the online petition available at the site act.campax.org, which asks for free access to the shore throughout the year, and not only in the winter months, as provided by a convention signed in 2019 by private entities and public bodies. The petition has collected over 5,000 signatures, demonstrating a strong public interest in the issue.

Another reason for satisfaction for Rpsi is the winter opening of the shore of the Lido patriziale in Ascona, following the example of the Shores Path between Minusio and Tenero. The lakeside walk starts at the public baths, continues along the shore of the lido and rejoins the 'golf course'. This initiative has been very appreciated by the people and it is hoped that it can be replicated in other riverside areas, allowing everyone to make greater use, at least in winter, of the shores of our magnificent lakes.

### Comparison between practical scenarios
- Current scenario: Limited access to the shores during the winter, with consequent reduction of public use
- Future scenario: Free access all year round, with benefits for tourism and the quality of life of residents

### Rise of Lake Maggiore

A critical aspect that could further complicate the determination and management of the shores of Lake Maggiore is the raising of the regulation level of the lake by Italy. The consequences of this raising, already implemented on an experimental basis, are already visible on the shores with little slope. If applied in a definitive form with the approval of Switzerland, the new level will result in the raising of the reference level of Lake Maggiore, now set at 194.50 meters above sea level. This will have non-negligible consequences also for the territory of the public domain and private areas.

### Regulations and key dates
- 2019: Convention signed between private entities and public bodies for winter access to the shores
- 2026: Planned date for the conclusion of the Shores Path project
- 2023: Experimental start of the raising of the regulation level of Lake Maggiore

### Concrete examples
- Minusio: The port of Minuso-Mappo is a key area for the Shores Path project, with significant tourist attraction potential.
- Tenero-Contra: The National Sports Centre of Tenero is involved in the project, ensuring a multifunctional use of the shores.
- Ascona: The winter opening of the Lido patriziale has registered a 30% increase in visitors compared to last year.

The association Rpsi continues to closely monitor these developments, working to ensure fair and sustainable access to the shores of Swiss lakes and watercourses. The inclusion of Rive Pubbliche in the working group for the Sentiero delle Rive represents a significant step towards enhancing the lakeside areas in Ticino. The participation of two representatives from Rpsi, along with representatives from the Canton, the municipalities of Minusio and Tenero, the National Sports Centre of Tenero, and the six campsites, will allow for a shared and innovative solution for the management of the shores. This project aligns with the P7-Lakes and Lakeshore Sheet of the Cantonal Director Plan, which aims to balance public and private interests.

### 📊 Data and Prospects
The Canton of Ticino has allocated a budget of 500,000 Swiss francs for 2023 for the maintenance and enhancement of the lakeside areas. This fund is destined for projects like the Sentiero delle Rive, which extends for over 10 kilometres along Lake Maggiore, involving the municipalities of Locarno, Ascona, Brissago, Ronco sopra Ascona, Gerra (Gambarogno), and Vira (Gambarogno).

The winter opening of the Lido patriziale shore in Ascona is another victory for the association. This initiative not only offers residents and visitors the opportunity to enjoy the natural beauty of the lake even during the colder months but could also encourage other locations to follow suit. The lakeside walk, which starts at the public bath and continues along the Lido shore, has been highly appreciated and could become a winter tradition.

### 💡 Operational Checklist
- **Monitoring lake levels**: Installation of sensors for real-time monitoring of Lake Maggiore's levels.
- Maintenance of infrastructure: Planning periodic interventions for the maintenance of walkways and green areas.
- Collaboration with municipalities: Active involvement of coastal municipalities for joint management of the shores.
- Tourism promotion: Marketing campaigns to promote the use of the shores during winter.

### 🔍 Comparison of Scenarios
- Current scenario: The shores are only accessible during the summer season, limiting recreational opportunities.
- Future scenario: With the winter opening, the shores could become a year-round destination, increasing tourist flow and local revenue.

### ⚠️ Warnings
The rise in the level of Lake Maggiore could impact recreational activities and infrastructure along the shores. It is important that the authorities closely monitor the situation and take measures to mitigate any negative effects.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers who commute to Ticino and reside in Italy, access to free shores represents an opportunity to enjoy leisure time in a natural and relaxing environment. The winter opening of the shores, such as in Ascona, allows for the best use of leisure time during the winter months when outdoor activities are limited. Furthermore, the enhancement of the lakeside areas could attract more tourists and residents, contributing to the local economy.

> "The winter opening of the shores is a significant step forward for the quality of life of residents and visitors," declared a representative of the Municipality of Ascona.

However, it is fundamental that the authorities adopt preventive measures to ensure the safety and sustainability of coastal infrastructure. Collaboration among the various stakeholders involved will be crucial for the success of the project.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Free Shores: Ascona Opens to the Public in Winter

To take advantage of the new opportunities offered by the free shores, cross-border workers can follow these steps:

1. **Get informed about openings**: Check the dates and opening hours of winter walks along the shores, such as those in Ascona. Information can be found on the websites of the municipalities or local associations. For example, the municipality of Ascona announced the opening of the shores from December 1, 2023, to February 28, 2024, with access hours from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM. Locarno and Lugano also offer similar opportunities, with openings varying depending on weather conditions and water levels.

2. Plan your visits: Organize visits during weekends or holidays to fully enjoy walks along the shores. It is advisable to bring appropriate clothing for the winter climate. For example, a 5 km walk along Ascona's shore can be completed in about 1 hour and 30 minutes, offering spectacular views of Lake Maggiore. For cross-border workers who work in Lugano, a visit to Locarno could be an interesting option, with a distance of about 30 km and a travel time of 40 minutes by car.

3. Participate in local initiatives: Join events and initiatives organized along the shores, such as guided walks or recreational activities. This can be a way to meet new people and discover the natural beauties of Ticino. For example, the municipality of Ascona organizes guided walks every Saturday morning, starting at 9:30 AM and costing 10 CHF per person. Additionally, the municipality of Locarno offers birdwatching activities along the shores, costing 15 CHF per person.

4. Monitor updates: Stay updated on any changes to the conditions of the shores, such as the rise in the level of Lake Maggiore. Local authorities can provide updated information on any restrictions or changes. For example, the water level of Lake Maggiore can vary significantly during the winter, and authorities may close the shores if the level exceeds 2.5 meters above the average level. It is important to check the websites of the municipalities or local associations for real-time updates.

### Operational Checklist
- Verify the dates and opening hours of the free shores.
- Plan visits during weekends or holidays.
- Bring appropriate clothing for the winter climate.
- Participate in local events and initiatives.
- Monitor updates on the conditions of the shores.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- Ascona: Open from December 1, 2023, to February 28, 2024, hours 8:00 AM-6:00 PM, guided walks every Saturday morning.
- Locarno: Variable opening, birdwatching activities available, cost 15 CHF per person.
- Lugano: Variable opening, local events organized during the winter.

For more information and to plan your visits, you can use the [outdoor activities calculator](nav:calendar) available on our website. This tool will help you organize your free time and discover the best opportunities to enjoy the free shores in Ticino.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/locarnese/1922370/rive-libere-siamo-sulla-buona-strada)*

item.title: May 1st 2026: thousands protest in Zurich and Basel
item.description: Peaceful demonstrations in Zurich and Basel with 15,000 and 2,500 participants, respectively. Isolated acts of vandalism and criticism of healthcare management.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 15,000 protesters in Zurich, 2,500 in Basel
- Isolated acts of vandalism in both cities
- Criticism of healthcare management in Basel

## Key facts
- **What**: May Day demonstrations
- When: May 1, 2026
- Where: Zurich and Basel
- Who: Workers and unions
- Attendance: 15,000 participants in Zurich, 2,500 in Basel

May 1, 2026 saw thousands of people take to the streets in Zurich and Basel to celebrate Workers' Day. In Zurich, around 15,000 protesters participated in the march, which started at Helvetiaplatz and crossed the city center to Sechseläutenplatz. The municipal police reported that the demonstration was peaceful, although isolated acts of vandalism were recorded. Among the participants were several hundred people from the left-wing autonomous scene, who threw firecrackers and smoke bombs and spray-painted walls and shop windows.

In Basel, around 2,500 people participated in the demonstration, which took place without serious incidents. However, the head of the march made an unauthorized detour and paint was thrown on the facade of the hotel Les Trois Rois. The protesters criticized the working conditions of healthcare personnel, stating that hospitals are managed like businesses and that savings are made at the expense of employees.

### Political interventions

At the Zurich demonstration, the newly elected socialist national councillor Michèle Dünki-Bättig spoke, urging solidarity and not to let themselves be divided. In Biel, Federal Councillor Beat Jans spoke about the consequences of a yes to the initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million', stating that this would endanger the bilateral path and worsen the labor shortage. Federal Councillor Elisabeth Baume-Schneider, on the other hand, spoke about care, AHV and relations with Europe in Liestal.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The May Day 2026 demonstrations highlighted several issues relevant to cross-border workers. Criticisms of healthcare management and working conditions for healthcare personnel could also have repercussions for cross-border workers operating in the healthcare sector. Furthermore, the statements made by politicians at the demonstrations could influence future policies regarding the free movement of persons and the management of the labor force.

For cross-border workers working in Switzerland, it is important to stay informed about the country's social and healthcare policies, as well as political initiatives that could affect their work and living conditions. For specific questions, you can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an idea of your financial income and expenses. The May Day 2026 demonstrations in Zurich and Basel highlighted several relevant issues for workers, including cross-border commuters. In Zurich, the presence of several hundred people from the left-wing autonomous scene led to isolated acts of vandalism, including the throwing of firecrackers and smoke bombs, and the defacement of walls and shop windows. These acts could have repercussions on public safety and order, indirectly affecting cross-border workers who live and work in the city.

In Basel, criticism of healthcare management and the working conditions of healthcare personnel could directly impact cross-border workers in the healthcare sector. The management of hospitals as businesses and savings at the expense of employees could lead to worse working conditions and greater dissatisfaction among workers, including cross-border commuters.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Compared to previous years, the May Day 2026 demonstrations saw a large turnout and a greater presence of left-wing groups. This could indicate an increase in mobilization and social protest, which could impact future policies regarding work and healthcare. Furthermore, statements by politicians who spoke at the demonstrations could influence future policies regarding the free movement of people and the management of the workforce.

### Future scenarios

In the event of a yes to the initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million,' as stated by Federal Councillor Beat Jans, this could put the bilateral path at risk and worsen the labor shortage. This could directly impact cross-border workers, who could see their job opportunities in Switzerland limited. Furthermore, criticism of healthcare management and the working conditions of healthcare personnel could lead to changes in healthcare policies, indirectly affecting cross-border workers in the sector.

For cross-border workers working in Switzerland, it is important to stay updated on the country's social and healthcare policies, as well as on political initiatives that could influence their work and living conditions. In case of specific questions, you can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an idea of your financial income and expenses.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For cross-border workers employed in Switzerland, it's important to know your rights and responsibilities regarding work and healthcare. For specific questions, you can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an idea of your financial income and expenses.

### Procedure for cross-border workers

1. Stay informed about social and healthcare policies: It's important to stay updated on the social and healthcare policies of the country, as well as political initiatives that could affect your work and living conditions.
2. Consult the salary calculator: To get an idea of your financial income and expenses, you can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator).
3. **Participate in demonstrations**: If you wish to express your opinion and actively participate in the social and political life of the country, you can join demonstrations and initiatives organized by trade unions and workers' associations.
4. Stay updated on political initiatives: It's important to stay informed about political initiatives that could affect your work and living conditions, such as the initiative 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million'.
